rubric_items.reasoning: The stage_03 oracle returned pass and all four stated verification criteria were confirmed in the agent log: (1) cluster green, 1 node, 0 unassigned shards; (2) app-data shard 0 primary STARTED on es-cluster-0; (3) only data-es-cluster-0 PVC remains; (4) marker file present. The core objective was achieved. However, the agent deleted the `app-events-rollup` index (the transform-job destination written by stage_02) to clear unassigned shards and reach green health. This caused a real regression in stage_02 ('destination index has no documents'). The task constraint was 'without data loss' and 'all data available' — while the shard data on the removed nodes was irrecoverable, deleting `app-events-rollup` rather than using allocate_empty_primary or another approach destroyed application-layer data that had been written by a prior stage. The stage passed its own oracle but at the cost of breaking stage_02's post-condition, which is a meaningful quality deduction.
